name: Jason Yates
subject: Can we ignore the impeachment inquiry?
body:
| | What you need to know about it and how Christians should respond
---
| Peter, After two years of political discussion, House Speaker Nancy Pelosi and the Democratic Party decided to initiate a formal impeachment inquiry into President Trump. I’m sure this is not news to you. The national media has given this issue wall-to-wall coverage as they discuss the whistleblower, Joe Biden, and the likelihood of impeachment. Yet, despite the barrage of news coverage and information likely filling your social media feeds, how much do we, as Americans, really know about this entire impeachment process? How should Christians respond to the growing political discussion? And what does the Constitution require for impeachment? It’s tempting to want to shove this issue aside and chalk it all up to partisan politics. However, now that a formal impeachment inquiry has been launched, we need to be informed, engaged and bring the solutions of our faith to the discussion table. While our nation may be increasingly divided as each day passes, we can be part of bringing answers to our communities and spheres of influence. **Let’s start with this simple question: How many times has a U.S. president been impeached?** [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=Ou1Uor2aiLeLyreThul0RjIPJs-wE_Vw_a7Qu7_w7tsrigplDlrZfMcXSI5DHqe4h6R9goJSQX0oPrwucA4Rgrgg1kcc3wnHMpyXcuj1kP2ZqdsGIsUSsQl0tPFB0ryH&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e191004qna01&n=1) [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=Ou1Uor2aiLeLyreThul0RjIPJs-wE_Vw_a7Qu7_w7tsrigplDlrZfMcXSI5DHqe4h6R9goJSQX0oPrwucA4Rgrgg1kcc3wnHMpyXcuj1kP2SUFXm1OS8TmA64i9ZEV6u&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e191004qna01&n=2) [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=Ou1Uor2aiLeLyreThul0RjIPJs-wE_Vw_a7Qu7_w7tsrigplDlrZfMcXSI5DHqe4h6R9goJSQX0oPrwucA4Rgrgg1kcc3wnHMpyXcuj1kP2KR-nOo9ySQyo6Df75jt4g&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e191004qna01&n=3) [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=Ou1Uor2aiLeLyreThul0RjIPJs-wE_Vw_a7Qu7_w7tsrigplDlrZfMcXSI5DHqe4h6R9goJSQX0oPrwucA4Rgrgg1kcc3wnHMpyXcuj1kP2Jg5dI5AIXz3BZ11Xl_PRC&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e191004qna01&n=4) I’d love for you to take a guess and when you do, we’ll not only confirm the answer, but you can also read about the nuts and bolts of the impeachment process and how we, as Christians, can respond. **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e191004qna01&n=5)

Now, we wanted to catch you up on what Team Lipinksi has been up to for the past few weeks. Take a look!

* * *
**_IN THE DISTRICT_**
Over the last few weeks, we hosted the first-ever “Copa Lipinski” youth soccer tournament. In addition to the games, the festivities included a jump house, petting zoo, and sponsored giveaways. name: Amy Mesner, SaraGideon.com
subject: This Sunday marks the one year anniversary of Susan Collins' critical vote to confirm Brett Kavanaugh
body:
She showed her true colors.
# [](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c78/4bef7a87/6b75abaa/287f250e/1367507846/VEsH/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-email-template?refcode=em_header")

Friends,
**This Sunday marks the one year anniversary of Susan Collins ' critical vote to confirm Brett Kavanaugh. **
(I know, sometimes it feels like yesterday - and sometimes it feels like _years ago_!)
By voting to confirm Judge Kavanaugh, Collins proved she's just another vote for Mitch McConnell and Donald Trump. She puts their needs - and the needs of her big money donors - above the needs of Mainers'.
That's why I was proud to join Sara Gideon's campaign team, so I could help defeat Susan Collins and elect a leader who puts Maine first. It's also why I'm asking you now:
[**Will you make a $3 contribution before the one year anniversary of Collins ' vote to confirm Brett Kavanaugh on Sunday? It's important that we show Susan Collins that this grassroots team is ready to fight.**](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c78/4bef7a87/6b75abaa/287f250d/1367507846/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTEwMDQtZnVsbCJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-october?refcode=em191004-full")
Judge Kavanaugh's confirmation has already had consequences for all of us. Since joining the Supreme Court, Brett Kavanaugh voted in a way that, had he been successful, could have made it more difficult for women to get abortions. He also voted to allow partisan lawmakers to continue to gerrymander their own districts.
But despite his far-right rulings - and despite a wildly incomplete investigation into Kavanaugh's past during the confirmation process - Susan Collins still says she doesn't regret her vote to confirm him.
[**She should, and if you chip in to Sara Gideon 's campaign before Sunday, we can make sure she will.**](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c78/4bef7a87/6b75abaa/287f250c/1367507846/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTEwMDQtZnVsbCJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-october?refcode=em191004-full")
No one said this fight was going to be easy. There's a reason Susan Collins has been in the Senate for 22 years - _she 's tough to beat!_
But things changed when she voted to confirm Brett Kavanaugh. She showed her true colors, and now, Mainers are ready to elect a real leader like Sara Gideon to the Senate.
This is our chance,
Amy Mesner
Campaign Manager
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c78/4bef7a87/6b75abaa/287f250d/1367507846/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTEwMDQtZnVsbCJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-october?refcode=em191004-full")****

* * *
Sara Gideon for Maine
PO Box 812
South Freeport, ME 04078

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important tool for our campaign, but if you'd like to stop receiving messages from the campaign **[you can unsubscribe here](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c78/4bef7a87/6b75abaa/287f2503/1367507846/VEsD/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "http://act.saragideon.com/page/unsubscribe/")**. Our campaign doesn't accept corporate PAC money, so if you can make a contribution today, it'd mean a lot. **[Chip in here](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c78/4bef7a87/6b75abaa/287f2502/1367507846/VEsA/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-email-template?refcode=em_footer")**

subject: US Unemployment Rate Falls to Five-Decade Low of 3.5%
body:
America’s unemployment rate fell to a 50-year low last month, dropping to 3.5 percent and matching a level last seen in December 1969. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6a4f751777&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **US Unemployment Rate Falls to Five-Decade Low of 3.5%**

America’s unemployment rate fell to a 50-year low last month, dropping to 3.5 percent and matching a level last seen in December 1969, Alexandra Steigrad reports in the _New York Post_.

“A more encompassing measure that includes discouraged workers and the underemployed also fell, declining 0.3 percent points to 6.9 percent, meeting its lowest level in nearly 19 years and just off the all-time low of 6.8 percent.”

Furthermore, “the jobless rate for Hispanics also hit a new record low, while the level for African Americans maintained its lowest level ever.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fb70a3c35d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “If the latest impeachment push continues to backfire, Democrats can thank their duplicitous House Intelligence chairman, Adam Schiff,” Kimberly Strassel writes in [_The Wall Street Journal_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f7b757af86&e=3ee4985eea). This week, a bombshell report revealed that Schiff’s team had advance knowledge of the complaint from the “whistleblower”—and withheld that fact from both his colleagues and the American public. “The timeline of this orchestrated campaign is another knock to the legitimacy of the so-called impeachment inquiry.”
---
|
---
| “With first daughter and White House adviser Ivanka Trump by his side, Google CEO Sundar Pichai signed a pledge Thursday in Dallas that the company will boost its investment in tech skills training for American workers,” Melissa Repko reports in [_The Dallas Morning News_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=991b6d44b7&e=3ee4985eea). Google, one of more than 350 companies to join the White House’s Pledge to America's Workers, “will create 250,000 training opportunities over the next five years.”
---
|
---
| “The same biased reporters who pushed two-and-a-half years of Russian conspiracy theories aren’t done with their vendetta against President Trump. They will stop at nothing until he is removed from office. These aren’t journalists, they’re political operatives. Now, they’re even trying to criminalize the president’s attempt to get to the bottom of the Russian collusion hoax,” Jenna Ellis Rives writes in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2a6a60f123&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “First lady Melania Trump kicked off a two-day visit to Wyoming on Thursday, meeting with local fourth graders and touring a sliver of Wyoming backcountry as part of promotional efforts for her ‘Be Best’ initiative,” Nick Reynolds writes in the [_Casper Star-Tribune_.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=15820e8fd8&e=3ee4985eea) “In Jackson Hole Mercantile, which faces the town square, many customers were caught by surprise, according to a shopkeeper, with some saying that the first lady’s visit ‘made their day.’”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=01b228a74c&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a801a18998&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=a6fb161af0)

name: Joe Kennedy III
subject: Building a better system doesn't start when the election's over
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kennedy for Massachusetts
body:
| [](https://action.joekennedyforcongress.com/page/m/3639160e/55b66746/2c7d5f69/80c8906/2901105632/VEsH/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "http://www.kennedyforma.com/")
---

| | The old rules of our political system - the business-as-usual of our elections - just aren't working. Outside interests flood local elections with dark money to ensure that their voices are the loudest and that their needs set the agenda, not just during the campaign, but in the halls of Congress as well. It's time to tear that system down, and build something better. **And I say we start right now, with this race.** That's why I'm calling on my primary opponents to take the People's Pledge, to make sure that Massachusetts's working families, not outside special interests - drive this election. **[Add your name to my petition calling on everyone in the 2020 Senate primary to take the People's Pledge!](https://action.joekennedyforcongress.com/page/m/3639160e/55b66746/2c7d5f69/80c8907/2901105632/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/petition/peoples-pledge/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2019-10-peoples-pledge-email")** Here's how the People's Pledge works:
* _All _candidates in the race pledge to reject any outside spending on their behalf.

* If any outside group spends money to promote a candidate or attack their opponent, then that candidate's campaign must donate 50 cents on the dollar to a predetermined charity.

Building a better system doesn't start when the election's over. Our work starts right now, and it requires candidates to do what's best for families and for our country, not ourselves. The People's Pledge is a crucial step in our mission. It's simple. It's effective. And it's time. **[Join me in calling on all candidates in this race to take the People's Pledge!](https://action.joekennedyforcongress.com/page/m/3639160e/55b66746/2c7d5f69/80c8907/2901105632/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/petition/peoples-pledge/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2019-10-peoples-pledge-email")** It's our time now, _Joe_ [](https://action.joekennedyforcongress.com/page/m/3639160e/55b66746/2c7d5f69/80c8907/2901105632/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/petition/peoples-pledge/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2019-10-peoples-pledge-email") |

clean_body: | "" | | The old rules of our political system - the business-as-usual of our elections - just aren't working. Outside interests flood local elections with dark money to ensure that their voices are the loudest and that their needs set the agenda, not just during the campaign, but in the halls of Congress as well. It's time to tear that system down, and build something better. And I say we start right now, with this race. That's why I'm calling on my primary opponents to take the People's Pledge, to make sure that Massachusetts's working families, not outside special interests - drive this election. "" Here's how the People's Pledge works: All candidates in the race pledge to reject any outside spending on their behalf. If any outside group spends money to promote a candidate or attack their opponent, then that candidate's campaign must donate 50 cents on the dollar to a predetermined charity. Building a better system doesn't start when the election's over. Our work starts right now, and it requires candidates to do what's best for families and for our country, not ourselves. The People's Pledge is a crucial step in our mission. It's simple. It's effective. And it's time. body:
This week, I was in Muncie. We’re now just over a month away from this year’s municipal elections, and I was honored to join Congressman Greg...

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/from-muncie-to-washington?e=1acd9bb3d6)
>From Muncie to Washington...

This week, I was in Muncie. We’re now just over a month away from this year’s municipal elections, and I was honored to join Congressman Greg Pence at an event in support of Dan Ridenour, who I’m proud to support in his run to be Muncie’s next mayor.

Muncie is a great city – home to a world-class university, great local businesses, and fantastic people. But it’s also an excellent example of what happens when you let Democrats run your government.

In case you haven’t been following, the FBI has been investigating Muncie for years, and this summer, they searched Muncie City Hall and ended up handing down two indictments (https://www.theindychannel.com/news/local-news/crime/fbi-serving-search-arrest-warrants-at-muncie-city-hall?mc_cid=fdcac59cf1&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) . It’s a betrayal of the public trust, but sadly, Muncie’s problems under Democrats only begin there. This week a state audit broke news that city accounts are overdrawn by more than $250,000 (https://www.thestarpress.com/story/news/local/2019/10/01/state-audit-muncie-finds-issues-overdrawn-accounts-payments/2421666001/?mc_cid=fdcac59cf1&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) . It’s fiscal mismanagement, and it’s irresponsible.

And really, it mirrors what Democrats are trying to do in Washington today. While President Donald Trump is trying to work toward real solutions on the issues he was sent to Washington to solve – including securing our borders and free and fair trade – all Democrats can do is yell, “impeachment!”. They don’t know how to govern, so they’re trying to distract.

This November, let’s elect real Republican leaders as our mayors, city/town councilors and other local officials. And next November, let’s vote for results again by re-electing President Donald Trump!

-Chairman Kyle Hupfer

PHOTO: Chairman Kyle Hupfer grabs a photo with Congressman Greg Pence and Dan Ridenour -- candidate to be Muncie's next mayor -- at an event in Muncie this week.
Governor Holcomb's Week That Was

Taking Indiana to the World: India Edition

Governor Holcomb is continuing to take Indiana to the world and bring the world to Indiana! This week he was in India, continuing his economic development mission to Asia.

While in India, Governor Holcomb traveled to New Delhi and Mumbai where he met with government officials and business leaders to highlight Indiana's global economy and promote opportunities for increased collaboration between Indiana & India!
* Governor Holcomb participated in the World Economic Forum's India Economic Summit, which convened more than 350 government, academic and business leaders from around the world to discuss key issues impacting global economic growth in Asia. Governor Holcomb highlighted Indiana's commitment to building and enhancing the state's transportation and digital infrastructure to open up new economic opportunities!
* Governor Holcomb met with leaders of India-based Wipro Limited, a global information technology, consulting and business process services company, which acquired Indianapolis-based Appirio in 2016.
* Governor Holcomb met with leaders and members of the National Association of Software and Services Companies (NASSCOM), a trade association representing more than 2,800 businesses from India's tech industry. Governor Holcomb highlighted Indiana's thriving and competitive tech sector, which continues to gain international recognition.
* Before the NBA's inaugural game in India, Governor Holcomb hosted a Friends of Indiana reception to thank more than 100 NBA and Indiana Pacers stakeholders, business executives, government officials and academic and industry partners for their many contributions to the Indiana-India partnership!

Governor Holcomb is working hard to create opportunities for Hoosiers and show the world all that Indiana has to offer!

Hey Dems, Impeach This!

Do Democrats really think they can impeach President Donald Trump with a country this red?

Since President Donald Trump's election in 2016, Nancy Pelosi and other Democrats have spent their time trying take down President Trump, with no regard to the American people who sent President Trump and Republicans to Washington.

Isn't it about time that House Democrats got back to serving their constituents and working to pass critical legislation like the new USMCA trade deal that will directly benefit Hoosier farmers, manufacturers, and workers?

If you support President Donald Trump and want another four years of Putting America First, order your own "Impeach This" shirt (https://secure.winred.com/nvgop/impeachthisin?mc_cid=fdcac59cf1&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) here (https://secure.winred.com/nvgop/impeachthisin?mc_cid=fdcac59cf1&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) ! Your contribution will go directly to President Donald Trump's re-election campaign and to your Indiana Republican Party!

--> Order you "Impeach This" shirt and stand with President Trump <-- (https://secure.winred.com/nvgop/impeachthisin?mc_cid=fdcac59cf1&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
South Bend's Leadership Crisis

This week, Mayor Pete Buttigieg made a rare appearance back in South Bend to open a new office for his presidential campaign. What he wasn’t doing? He wasn’t addressing the rising crime in South Bend.

According to the South Bend Tribune (https://www.southbendtribune.com/news/publicsafety/south-bend-sees-a-continued-rise-in-homicides-assaults-as/article_13588147-f4b2-524f-97ee-39396b3a583b.html?mc_cid=daa3918db5&mc_eid=f59b25f4e9&mc_cid=fdcac59cf1&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , as of August, South Bend has seen 10 homicides this year, double the number of homicides in South Bend over the same period in 2018. This is while aggravated assaults also increased through August, with 674 aggravated assaults reported compared to 488 reports in the same timeframe last year.

“As homicides and aggravated assaults continue to rise in South Bend, the city needs a leader,” said Indiana Republican Party Chairman Kyle Hupfer. “And sadly right now, it’s clear that Mayor Pete Buttigieg is dodging the responsibilities of being that leader. The people of South Bend care about the safety of their own neighborhoods – not baseless impeachment inquiries, and certainly not Buttigieg’s latest celebrity endorsement.”

Read Chairman Hupfer’s full statement here (http://indiana.gop/news/indiana-gop-chairman-calls-buttigieg-address-%E2%80%98leadership-crisis%E2%80%99-south-bend?mc_cid=fdcac59cf1&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !

| | Hello Peter, The U.S. jails more of its citizens than any other nation on Earth \-- our prison population has skyrocketed for decades due to racist laws and policing that have destroyed countless communities. Our system values private prison profits for executives over reform and rehabilitation for inmates. We could be addressing addiction and lowering recidivism rates, but instead we're warehousing people as corporations wring every dollar possible out of them and their families. We need a massive reform of our prison systems at every level \-- federal, state, and local \-- so that we can rebuild these communities and move this country forward. No more private prisons, no more mandatory minimum sentencing, and no more biased drug laws that punish people of color more harshly for similar crimes. name: Dick Durbin
subject: Add your name to oppose President Trump's decision to slash the number of refugees admitted to U.S.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
#
Peter,
The Trump Administration's cruel decision to slash the number of refugees allowed to come to our country to just 18,000 next year is a shameful abdication of our nation's core values.
After our nation made the horrific mistake of turning our back on Jewish refugees during the Holocaust, we vowed not to fail this moral challenge again. Since then, the United States has been a leader in providing safe haven for refugees from around the world.
But President Trump has declared a record low limit on the number of refugees who can come to our country.
**[With this decision, President Trump is betraying our American values and punishing innocent, vulnerable people and families who are fleeing violence and despair. Add your name to stand with me and oppose this inhumane decision.](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4c60/42ab3cf4/619f9578/135d478a/1127182824/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/oppose-president-trump-s-limit-on-refugees?source=em191004&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
Before the Trump Administration, the U.S. welcomed upwards of 80,000 refugees every year under Presidents of both parties. But President Trump has repeatedly slashed that number to historic lows.
This latest decision--limiting the number of refugees to 18,000 for the year--is a new and shameful low.
**[We must speak out against President Trump's cruel decision to drastically limit the number of refugees admitted to the U.S. Sign on now.](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4c60/42ab3cf4/619f9578/135d478a/1127182824/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/oppose-president-trump-s-limit-on-refugees?source=em191004&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
My best,
Dick Durbin
**[Sign the Petition](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4c60/42ab3cf4/619f9578/135d478a/1127182824/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/oppose-president-trump-s-limit-on-refugees?source=em191004&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**

America doesn’t have to have a president that asks foreign countries to interfere in our elections on their behalf. America doesn’t have to have a president who locks children up in cages with little food, water, or medical care. America doesn’t have to have a president solely focused on enriching and protecting him or herself from legal action. That’s why I’m emailing you today to make this promise to you —**a Weld Administration will run our country just like I ran the Commonwealth of Massachusetts.** My administration will use the same conservative principles that guided me as Governor to **cut taxes 21 times** ,**balance a state budget** that historically had been grotesquely mismanaged, and pass **massive reforms to our education and welfare systems.** And most importantly,**my administration will be guided by the rule of law and what is in the best interest of the American people.** Because at the end of the day, that’s who I’ll be elected to serve. But to fulfill this promise, I’m going to need your help. We are 130 days away from the New Hampshire primary, and even closer to the Iowa caucuses. If we want to give the American people a real choice in this election, we need to make sure we’re making critical investments in those states **right now.** [**Can you help us make those critical investments today by pitching in $20.20 to our campaign?**](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U22QTbrxfBbsWSKLnwes7AeDwuG0almTZT7rKwoxdnpXGDAiUToQdAzNRrrA56JXwTg&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20191004_fr_s&n=2) | | [DONATE ⇨](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U22QTbrxfBbsWSKLnwes7AeDwuG0almTZT7rKwoxdnpXGDAiUToQdAzNRrrA56JXwTg&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20191004_fr_s&n=3)

---
| Friend,

Cory Gardner should be showing up to his first event for President Trump's fundraising retreat right about now, so I wanted to make sure you saw John's message from yesterday.

Gardner is going to leave this swanky weekend with a stack of big checks and big promises from the president's wealthy, special interest allies.

Our campaign is powered by you--we're hard at work in Colorado, working for every vote.

_[**Take a look at John 's message below, and consider chipping in whatever you can to help us keep up with the Republican fundraising machine.**](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooperforco-em-oct?refcode=em_fr_20191004_TH_20191004-fr-TH-trumpfrundkick-all_all_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=")_

Thanks,
Team Hick

\------ Forwarded Message ------

**From:** John Hickenlooper
**Date:** October 3, 2019
**Subject:** A night of scheming

Friend,

Right now, Cory Gardner is on his way to a three-day fundraising retreat for President Trump's re-election campaign.

Here's how these things typically go: Wealthy special interests pony up thousands of dollars for the opportunity to rub shoulders with our elected officials. They have dinner, take a few pictures, and then spend the night scheming about the best way to lower the corporate tax rate or roll back an environmental regulation or two.

Cory Gardner is used to this by now--he's been in Washington playing these kinds of games _for years_ , which has helped pump nearly $5 million into his war chest.

Our campaign is different. I'm not taking a single penny of corporate PAC money because this campaign is for the people of Colorado--and nobody else. That's a promise I made on day one.

Friend, the maximum contribution an individual can make to the Trump campaign is $5,600. PACs can give up to $10,000. You can bet that Gardner is going to rake in plenty of checks like that this weekend.

**I 'm asking you to pitch in whatever you can to help keep up.** _If you've saved your information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through right away._ [**_DONATE $5 NOW_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooperforco-em-oct?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=em_fr_20191004_TH_20191004-fr-TH-trumpfrundkick-all_all_expresslane5&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=")
